title: Steps from Post-Parity State to Finished, Ready-to-Use Registry System

state: Tooling is deterministic: two builds byte-identical, --check covers every managed output, manifest verifies, Python 3.9/3.11 green.
Structural validation and parity regression are blocking in CI.
Parity report exists with a frozen baseline of known divergences.
All 10 registries carry a coverage classification; 6 are no_declared_authority.
22 contract review items, 25 governance conflicts, 2 orphan classifications, and 3+ stub modules remain unresolved.

definition_of_finished.statement: The registry system is finished when it is the authoritative source of truth its consumers actually use, and the documents it replaced are formally superseded.
criteria: Every registry is either canonical (cut over) or explicitly retired with a recorded decision.
Parity baseline is empty for every cut-over registry.
Zero needs_review records; zero unresolved conflict-queue entries; zero undispositioned orphans.
No stub records: every module record has responsibilities, process_step_ids, and confidence high.
All validation is blocking; report-only mode is removed from CI.
At least one downstream consumer derives artifacts from registry data in CI, proving the system is used, not just correct.
Superseded documents are marked superseded in doc_authority.json and carry pointer headers to their replacing registry.
An operator runbook exists for adding, changing, and retiring registry records.

steps.name: Triage the parity baseline into work classes
steps.description: Classify every baselined divergence into exactly one disposition class: registry_defect (fix the registry record), authority_stale (authority is wrong; open governance issue to update it), adapter_gap (comparison rule wrong; fix tooling), or accepted_difference (documented, deliberate). No fixes yet; classification only.
outputs: Baseline with every entry dispositioned (no 'unreviewed' remaining)
One tracking issue per registry with per-class counts
steps.governance_note: authority_stale items require governance approval before the authority document may change; the registry never wins by default.

steps.name: Resolve contract divergences and the 22 contract review items together
steps.description: Contracts are the highest-risk area. For each conflicted contract: identify producer and consumer modules, determine the actual interface from code/authority evidence, correct the contract record, record the evidence in source_refs, and clear the needs_review flag. Where two modules genuinely disagree about their interface, that is a real integration defect — open a code issue, do not paper over it in the registry.
outputs: Baseline entries for contract_registry removed as resolved
Code issues for any genuine interface mismatch discovered
steps.done_when: contract_registry: needs_review = 0, conflict queue empty for contracts, parity baseline empty for contract_registry

steps.name: Populate stub module records
steps.description: Complete every module record flagged confidence: medium or with empty responsibilities/process_step_ids (R3_CORRELATION_GUARD, U1_DASHBOARD_BACKEND, U2_GUI_GATEWAY, and any others). Facts must be extracted from code and authority documents with source_refs, never invented. If a module genuinely has no implementation yet, record it explicitly as planned status rather than leaving fields empty.
outputs: module_registry.jsonl with all 34 records at confidence high
needs_review_fields empty across the module registry
steps.done_when: 0 stub records; module parity baseline empty

steps.name: Disposition orphans and close the conflict queue
steps.description: Resolve the 2 orphan classifications (including SK1_PLUGIN_INTERFACE / SK2_IDEMPOTENCY reclassification if warranted) and every remaining item in registry_conflict_queue.jsonl and the 25-item review set. Every closure carries evidence and lands as a decision_registry record where it reflects an architectural judgement.
steps.done_when: conflict queue empty; 0 orphans undispositioned; the 25 review items each closed with recorded disposition

steps.name: Establish an authority basis for the six no_declared_authority registries
steps.description: integration, configuration, operational_control, operator, reuse, and decision registries cannot cut over on parity evidence because nothing external verifies them. For each, choose exactly one path: (a) nominate an existing document as its parity authority via doc_authority.json and add the mapping + adapter, (b) declare the registry born-canonical because no prior document exists — verified instead by targeted evidence checks (e.g. configuration records verified against actual config files in the repo; operational controls against deployed risk-control code), or (c) retire the registry as not needed, recorded in decision_registry.
outputs: New adapters and evidence validators as required
decision_registry records for every born-canonical or retirement decision

steps.name: Close schema gaps (unrepresentable) and comparison gaps (not_comparable)
steps.description: For every unrepresentable verdict: extend the registry schema so the fact can be expressed, then populate it. For every not_comparable field: either define a deterministic normalization that makes it comparable, or record an accepted disposition with written rationale. Schema changes go through the normal schema + validator + regeneration cycle.
steps.done_when: unrepresentable = 0 for all cutover-track registries; every not_comparable field dispositioned

steps.name: Prove sustained green parity per registry
steps.description: For each registry meeting the cutover criteria (baseline empty, authority_only = 0, field_divergence = 0, unrepresentable = 0, registry_only dispositioned), hold it green across the declared minimum consecutive commits (recommend 10) with normal repository activity continuing. This proves stability under change, not a lucky snapshot.
steps.done_when: Per-registry green-streak counter reaches threshold, tracked in a generated stability report

steps.name: Execute per-registry authority cutover
steps.description: For each qualified registry, in dependency order (module and process first, since contract/artifact reference them): record governance approval in decision_registry; set authority_status canonical and approved_for_cutover true in registry_index.json for that registry only; update doc_authority.json to mark the predecessor document superseded-by-registry; add a superseded banner + pointer to the top of the predecessor document; flip that registry's advisory findings to blocking in CI.
steps.done_when: Each target registry canonical in registry_index.json with supersession recorded
steps.rollback: Cutover is a metadata flip; rollback = revert the registry_index/doc_authority commit. Predecessor documents are retained superseded, never deleted, precisely so rollback and audit remain possible.

steps.name: Remove report-only mode and retire shadow scaffolding
steps.description: Once all cutover-track registries are canonical: delete continue-on-error and --report-only paths from CI for those registries, rename the workflow to drop '(Shadow)', and make the full validation suite (structural + parity + authority-state) a required check for merge. The parity job remains permanently — it now guards against the canonical registries drifting from any remaining authoritative documents and from each other.
steps.done_when: CI has no advisory escape hatch for canonical registries; validation is a required branch-protection check

steps.name: Wire the first downstream consumers
steps.description: A correct registry nobody reads is not finished. Convert at least the first consumers from hand-authored to registry-derived: (a) generated architecture docs replace their hand-written counterparts, (b) CI derives the module dependency graph for build ordering / impact analysis from module_registry, (c) AI agent context packs (routing instructions, module briefs) are generated from registry data instead of maintained by hand, (d) implementation contracts for new work are instantiated from contract_registry records.
outputs: At least one CI job that consumes registry .current.json snapshots as input
Generated agent-context artifacts with a build-manifest provenance chain
steps.done_when: Deleting a registry record visibly breaks a downstream consumer's CI — proof of real dependency

steps.name: Establish the change workflow and runbook
steps.description: Document and enforce the steady-state lifecycle: how a record is added (authored JSONL edit -> schema/FK validation -> regeneration -> PR with generated diff reviewed together), how a record is changed, how a record is retired (status transition per the authority-state matrix, never deletion), and how a new registry is introduced (index entry, schema, adapters, baseline). Add PR templates and a CODEOWNERS rule for 01_canonical_registries/.
outputs: CODEOWNERS coverage for authored registry paths
steps.done_when: A new record can be added end-to-end by following the runbook alone, gated entirely by existing CI

steps.name: Final supersession sweep and declaration of done
steps.description: Audit every legacy architecture/contract/process document against doc_authority.json: each is either (a) superseded with pointer, (b) still authoritative for facts no registry covers (recorded), or (c) archived with a decision record. Publish a final system status report generated from registry data itself, and record the completion in decision_registry.
steps.done_when: definition_of_finished criteria all satisfied; completion decision recorded

parallelizable: STEP-02, STEP-03, STEP-04, STEP-06 after triage
STEP-05 can start immediately, in parallel with all burn-down work
STEP-10 and STEP-11 in parallel after first cutovers

invariants_for_all_steps: The remediation plan's determinism guarantees are never regressed: two builds byte-identical, --check total, manifest verified.
Every registry data change carries source_refs evidence; no fact is invented to satisfy a validator.
Authority documents change only through governance approval, never to make parity pass.
The baseline only shrinks by resolution, never by silent regeneration.
Cutover is always per-registry and always reversible via metadata revert.
Predecessor documents are superseded, never deleted.

risk_register.risk: Burn-down stalls because contract conflicts reveal real code-level integration defects
risk_register.mitigation: STEP-02 explicitly splits registry correction from code fixes; registry records the true (conflicting) state and the code issue tracks resolution. Cutover for contract_registry can proceed with dispositioned registry_only records even while code issues remain open.

risk_register.risk: Born-canonical registries (STEP-05 path b) become unverified assertion islands
risk_register.mitigation: Path b requires substitute evidence validators (config records vs actual config files, etc.) so every registry has some external check, even without a predecessor document.

risk_register.risk: Consumers never materialize and the system decays into well-validated shelfware
risk_register.mitigation: STEP-10's done_when is deliberately harsh: a registry deletion must break consumer CI. If no consumer can be wired, that is a signal to revisit whether the registry earns its maintenance cost.

risk_register.risk: Post-cutover drift between canonical registries and reality (code)
risk_register.mitigation: STEP-09 keeps parity permanent, and STEP-10's derived artifacts mean drift surfaces as consumer breakage rather than silent staleness.
